semanticCore: Frowning, showing the teeth, distorted lips in pain, or grimacing under intense heat

keyDerivations.meaning: To look grim, show the teeth with contracted lips, or grimace in severe pain

figurativeOrRareUsages: تَلْفَحُ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ وَهُمْ فِيهَا كَالِحُونَ (23:104): The Fire will scorch their faces, and they therein will be grinning/grimacing (with contracted lips exposing their teeth).
summaryEn: The root denotes facial distortion and grimacing due to physical agony (Kalh), illustrating the severe torment of Hell where fire contracts the lips of the damned.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر تكشر الوجه وتقلص الشفتين عن الأسنان عند احتراق الجلد والعذاب الشديد (كلح)، واصفاً بؤس وجوه أهل النار وقد شوهتها النار الكالحة.
rawText: أَكْلَحَهُ 4 He (or it, L) made him to grin, or display his teeth, frowning, or contracting his face, or looking sternly, austerely, or morosely. (L, K.) === See 1. بَلَآءٌ مُكْلِحٌ A trial, or an affliction, which, by its severity, makes men grin and frown. (L, from a trad.) تكلّح 5 ‡ He smiled: see 1. (K.) === Hence, (TA,) تكلّح البَرْقُ ‡ The lightning flashed in continued succession: (S, L, K:) also, it continued, and became concealed, in a white cloud. (L.) كلّح وَجْهَهُ 2 He contracted his face much. (A.) كَالِحٌ , act. part. n. of 1. === Also, Having the lip withdrawn from the teeth. (Zj, L.) So in the Kur xxiii. 106, accord. to Zj. (L.) ___ دَهْرٌ كَالِحٌ ‡ Severe, distressing, or afflictive, fortune, or time; (S, K;) as also 🡻كُلَاحٌ. (TA.) كَلَاحِ : see كُلَاحٌ. كَلَحَ 1 , aor. ﹷ, inf. n. كُلُوحٌ and كُلَاحٌ; (S, K;) and 🡻تلكّح, and 🡻اكلح, (K,) and 🡻اكلوّح; (A;) He (a man, S) grinned, or displayed his teeth, (M, rendered in the S and K by تَكَشَّرَ), frowning, or contracting his face, or looking sternly, austerely, or morosely. (S, M, K.) === كَلَحَ فِى وَجْهِهِ He frightened him; namely a child, and a madman. (A.) كَلَحَةٌ ‡ The mouth and parts around it. So in the phrase مَا أَقْبَحَ كَلَحَتَهُ How ugly is his mouth with the parts around it! (S, K.) كَوْلَحٌ Foul, unseemly, or ugly; syn. قَبِيحٌ: (K;) an epithet applied to a man. (TA.) كُلَاحٌ (S, K) and 🡻كَلَاحِ, the latter [indecl.] like قَطَامِ, (K,) ‡ A year of dearth, scarcity, drought, sterility, or barrenness. (S, K.) You say أَصَابَتْهُمْ سَنَةٌ كُلَاحٌ A year of dearth, &c., befell them. (TA.) See كَالِحٌ. مُكَالَحَةٌ 3 [inf. n. of كالحهُ He contended with him for superiority in strength;] i. q. مُشَادَّةٌ. (S.) [And so مُجَالَحَةٌ.]
